summarization.a: The figure shows four ab initio MD  illustrating the transfer of the amide group of the densified HfX₂ precursor to an under-coordinated hafnium atom at the HfO₂ surface. The panels capture the dynamic progression of ligand rearrangement and bonding as the system evolves at 500 K over 0.4 ps. These configurations highlight surface–ligand interactions central to precursor behavior.

d.question: Why is understanding the amide transfer mechanism shown in Figure 9 important for ALD/PEALD precursor design?
d.answer: Understanding the amide transfer mechanism is crucial because it reveals how precursor ligands interact with under-coordinated metal atoms on oxide surfaces during growth. This directly influences film quality, ligand removal efficiency, and surface reactivity, all of which are key parameters in ALD and PEALD. The snapshots in Figure demonstrate the dynamic behavior of the precursor under realistic thermal conditions, showing how ligands detach, reorganize, and attach to surface sites. Such mechanistic insight helps in designing more efficient precursors with improved volatility, reactivity, and cleanliness of by-products, ultimately enabling better control over film uniformity and composition.

b.question: Which panels show the initial and final stages of the amide transfer during the ab initio MD sequence?
b.answer: Panel (a) represents the initial configuration, while panel (d) shows the advanced stage where the amide group is coordinated to the under-coordinated Hf.

a.question: Does Figure show the transfer of an amide group from the densified HfX₂ precursor to an under-coordinated hafnium atom during the MD simulation?

c.question: What structural changes occur in the precursor–surface system during the amide transfer?
c.answer: Partial detachment of the amide group from the precursor, Increasing interaction between the amide nitrogen and under-coordinated surface Hf, Reorganization of coordination bonds around the surface Hf atom, Gradual stabilization of the ligand in the final configuration.
